refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall minimize cycle time by optimizing load, travel, and dump operations to meet the customer-defined cycle time target.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is broad; refining it into a focus on minimizing cycle time through the key operations clarifies the functional scope and enables targeted design.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure cycle time p95 under representative duty cycles and site conditions to verify achievement of the target.

candidate_dps: DP2: Real-time cycle time monitoring and feedback control
DP3: Efficient vehicle routing and path planning

potential_sources: Interdependence between load, travel, and dump operations timing
coupling_check.notes: The adaptive scheduling algorithm logically decouples the operations by dynamically adjusting timing, minimizing tight coupling.

assumptions_risks: Assumes accurate and timely telemetry data from vehicle and environment
Risk of algorithm complexity impacting real-time responsiveness
Safety criticality requires fail-safe fallback modes
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating real-time monitoring DP if adaptive scheduling alone does not meet cycle time targets.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Implements the adaptive operation scheduling algorithm coordinating load, travel, and dump cycle timing.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives real-time vehicle and environment data to inform scheduling decisions.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Sends operation timing commands to Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Supports communication between NGHE Solution and other modules for coordination.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment as scheduling logic is centralized here; low diffusion risk.
Vehicle module executes commands but does not contain scheduling logic.
Teleoperation receives commands but does not implement scheduling.
Safety Services monitors but does not control cycle time.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing scheduling DP in NGHE Solution module minimizes diffusion and improves maintainability.
containment_risks: Potential bottleneck if NGHE Solution module becomes too complex
Dependency on reliable telemetry and network link interfaces
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Monitor complexity growth in NGHE Solution; consider modularizing scheduling sub-functions if needed.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP (adaptive scheduling algorithm) logically satisfies the refined FR by targeting cycle time optimization across load, travel, and dump operations.

diffusion_risks: Low diffusion risk due to centralized scheduling logic in NGHE Solution module.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores support that NGHE Solution is the best architectural boundary for the DP.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification via telemetry data and cycle time measurements is feasible with current interfaces.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of scheduling DP to NGHE Solution module with well-defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Track telemetry data quality and latency as it impacts scheduling effectiveness.
Plan for fallback modes in case of network or telemetry failures.

decision.justification: The functional decomposition level is complete with clear FR refinement, DP selection, and IM assignment meeting containment and coupling criteria. Verification and architectural clarity are sufficient to proceed.

revise.why: No immediate revisions needed; next step is to decompose sub-FRs in subsequent levels.

assumptions: Telemetry data is accurate and timely
Safety criticality is addressed by separate Safety Services module
open_risks: Potential telemetry or network failures affecting scheduling
evidence_needed: Cycle time measurements under representative duty cycles
Telemetry data latency and accuracy reports
